programmes.show_description: A Thin Time: Sitcom about a two-man bus crew. Arthur is attracted to Beryl the clippie, but Beryl has only two interests in life - athletic men and those with a good head of hair.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Kung Who: Comedy series about two rival antique dealers. Oliver accepts Simon's challenge to take up judo - and soon regrets his decision.
The Prize: Classic sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Everyone wants to go when Mum wins a holiday for two to the Costa Brava.
Charity Begins at Home: Sitcom. Mike urges his parents to spend a weekend living on bread and water and giving the money saved on food to charity, but Sid finds it quite difficult.
Wives and Lovers: Classic '70s sitcom with Sid James. Jean hatches a plan to catch Sid out when she gets worried about the many flirty women that he meets in his line of work.
I'm Not Jealous, I'll Kill Him!: Classic sitcom. An old flame from Jean's youth comes back into her life and asks her out to dinner - Sid is surprisingly fine with this... or is he?
The Unkindest Cut of All: Domestic sitcom spin-off from the show Man About the House. George is ordered to watch his table manners when Mildred's sister comes round for dinner.
Comedy spin-off film sequel starring Harry H Corbett and Wilfrid Brambell, 1973. Albert must die when things get very bad at the Steptoes' Shepherd's Bush rag-and-bone business.

programmes.show_name: Carry On... Don't Lose Your Head
programmes.show_description: Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Jim Dale, 1967. Two English noblemen decide to rescue their French counterparts from the revolting masses and Madame Guillotine.
Bawdy British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1973. The town council of a seaside resort are persuaded to hold a beauty contest with chaotic results.
Bawdy British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1974. 1750 - a newly-formed police force attempt to catch Dick Turpin, the most notorious of all highwaymen.
Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Kenneth Williams, 1972. A package holiday on the island of Elsbels turns into a hilarious adventure rife with innuendo and gags.
Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1972. A gang plans to raid a maternity hospital in order to steal a gigantic stock of birth control pills.
Driving Mr McLynn: Comedy-drama series. Martin is shocked when Louisa reveals that she wants to be the school headteacher again, and Pauline worries about Al's secretive behaviour.
The Departed: Comedy-drama series. A visit for Martin to London is disturbed on the way back home by the death of a local sheep farmer whose widow starts to see visions of him.
Dark Autumn: Feature-length drama. DCI Barnaby uncovers a hotbed of adultery within a remote hamlet when a postman is brutally murdered - can a community WPC help find the killer?
The Pale Horse: After a friend is murdered, Miss Marple vows to seek justice. A clue leads her to an inn run by modern-day witches who claim to be able to kill using black magic.
Bye Bye Blakey: Classic sitcom about a bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan and Jack eavesdrop on a medical Blakey is having, but they end up jumping to the wrong conclusions.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
On Account: Detective drama series. Wycliffe and Kersey exchange strong words as they investigate a disturbing case involving the contamination of jars of baby food in a supermarket.
Lone Voyager: Drama series about a Cornwall police detective. Wycliffe and the team find themselves dealing with more than a missing persons case when a lone yachtswoman disappears.
Fearful Symmetry: When a babysitter is found dead, Lewis and Hathaway are drawn into diverse new worlds including squats, swinging suburbia, primate labs and fetish photography.
4:50 from Paddington: When Elspeth McGillicuddy sees a woman on a train being strangled, the authorities think that she has been dreaming, but Miss Marple knows better...
Driving Mr McLynn: Comedy-drama series. Martin is shocked when Louisa reveals that she wants to be the school headteacher again, and Pauline worries about Al's secretive behaviour.
Up the Garden Path: The organiser of a Garden Open Day Scheme calls on Rosemary and Laura to save the good name of her village when she discovers that its gardens are blighted.
Down Among the Dead Men: When a council clerk is shot dead, Barnaby and Jones discover that he was a blackmailer and investigate which of his victims hated him enough to kill him.
Death in Chorus: When Midsomer Worthy's baritone Connor Simpson is killed just days before a choir competition, the race is on for Barnaby and Jones to catch his killer.
Country Matters: Controversial plans to build a supermarket in Elverton-cum-Latterly lead to murder when a man is stabbed - can Barnaby and Jones uncover the killer?
Last Year's Model: Barnaby begins to question the guilt of a woman who is on trial for the murder of her best friend when the prosecution's claims do not appear to add up.
The Escape Turn: Crime drama series. Vera investigates when a wealthy betting shop magnate is shot and killed in front of his wife and daughter during a terrifying home invasion.
Dead End: DI Frost is called to the scene of a kidnapping where the driver and conductor of a Denton bus have been captured in a revenge plot planned by two grief-stricken victims.
